title: cuscuta epithymum var. brevistyla
intro:
Cuscuta epithymum var. brevistyla, also known as Dodder, is a parasitic flowering plant that grows by attaching itself to its host plant and obtaining nutrients through the host's vascular system.

This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.

The health benefits of Cuscuta epithymum var. brevistyla include its ability to improve cardiovascular health, reduce blood pressure, and boost the immune system. 

In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
